scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones (Bb, D, F) plus safe extensions (C, G). Zero avoid notes. Extremely common in jazz horn arrangements.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root (Bb) and major 3rd (D) for resolution. Perfect for jazz, R&B, and horn sections.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The go-to scale for Bb major in jazz, R&B, pop, and classical. Bb is one of the most common keys for horns and wind instruments.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental Bb major scale with two flats (Bb, Eb). Complete melodic freedom. One of the most common keys in jazz and classical.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. The Eb (11th) can sound suspended - resolve to D or F.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Bb major. Essential for jazz standards, classical music, and horn arrangements.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Raises Eb to E (#11) creating dreamy, ethereal quality. Very common in modern jazz over Bbmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 (E) for signature Lydian color. Creates uplifting, modern sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Bbmaj7#11 or modern I major sounds. Essential for contemporary jazz and film music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lowers A to Ab (b7) creating dominant 7th character. Bb7 is extremely common in jazz and blues.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The Ab (b7) creates bluesy edge. Bb7 is a staple in jazz and R&B.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for Bb7 chords in jazz, blues, and R&B. Essential for dominant vamps and horn sections.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Built on V - creates bluesy major sound
scaleCompatibility.rationale: All notes diatonic to Bb major. Classic blues-rock approach over major chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Creates bluesy major without clash. Perfect for rock guitar over Bb major.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential rock guitar scale over Bb major. Safe and bluesy.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Bbmaj7 is one of the most common chords in jazz standards.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7 (A). All tones work beautifully over Bbmaj7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Bbmaj7. Extremely common in jazz standards and R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (E) avoids suspended sound. Bbmaj7#11 is a jazz staple. This IS the modern Imaj7 sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (E) is signature sound. Bbmaj7#11 is essential in modern jazz vocabulary.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive modern Imaj7 scale. Essential for jazz, fusion, and contemporary music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Omits 4th and 7th for core consonance. Simple approach to maj7 harmony.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and melodic. Perfect for pop and R&B over Bbmaj7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for pop and R&B maj7 approaches. Creates smooth, singable melodies.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C Dorian is the natural ii mode in Bb major. The major 6th (A) creates brighter, jazzier minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the A (13th) for Dorian character. Essential for ii-V-I progressions.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive ii chord scale in Bb major. Essential for jazz, R&B, and contemporary music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Natural minor with Ab instead of A. Creates traditional, somber minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The Ab (b13) creates darker color. Use for traditional minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for darker ii chord sound. More melancholic than Dorian.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Safe minor pentatonic removes complexity. Universal minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and simple. Perfect for straightforward minor melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple, bluesy ii minor. Works in R&B and rock contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. A (13th) essential for ii-V-I. Cm7-F7-Bbmaj7 is a jazz standard progression.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: A (13th) crucial for Dorian color. Smooth voice leading to F7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for Cm7-F7-Bbmaj7. The standard iim7 sound in jazz and R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Bb major reinforces key center. All notes work since Cm7 is diatonic to Bb.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize C as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for key center emphasis. Great for teaching diatonic harmony.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: D Phrygian is the iii mode of Bb major. The b2 (Eb) creates Spanish/exotic character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 (Eb) for Phrygian color. Creates dark, mysterious quality.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced iii minor. Creates drama in Bb major.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: All Bb major notes work over Dm. Maintains key center.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize D as root. Simple diatonic approach.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center over iii. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Eb major is the natural scale for IV in Bb major. Creates subdominant function.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize root (Eb) and major 3rd (G). Ab (11th) can sound suspended.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V progressions in Bb. Perfect for jazz, classical, and R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (A) creates modern, floating IV sound. Common in jazz.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (A) creates bright, dreamy quality. Perfect for Ebmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern IV sound. Perfect for jazz and contemporary music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Bb major over IV maintains key center. All notes work.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize Eb as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for diatonic IV approach. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural V chord mode in Bb major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: F Mixolydian shares all notes with Bb major. The b7 (Eb) creates dominant 7th character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The Eb (b7) creates dominant pull to Bb. Emphasize A and Eb for strong V-I.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7 chords in Bb major. Perfect for dominant-tonic resolution.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: F major provides pure V triad. E (maj7) is brighter than Mixolydian's Eb.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More classical V approach. E creates major 7th rather than dominant 7th.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical, traditional V sound.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Bb major contains all V chord tones. F Mixolydian = Bb Ionian starting on F.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize F as root. Eb creates natural dominant character.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic V approach. Maintains key center.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones perfectly. F7 is extremely common in jazz and blues in Bb.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize 3rd (A) and b7 (Eb) - the tritone that resolves to Bb.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I (Cm7-F7-Bbmaj7).

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Bb major over F7 maintains key center. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize F as root. Eb creates dominant 7th automatically.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key awareness over V7.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Adds blue notes for authentic blues dominant. The b5 (B) creates blues tension.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use B (b5) as passing tone to C. Creates authentic blues sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for blues in Bb. Perfect for 12-bar blues.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Relative minor - shares all notes with Bb major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: G minor is the vi of Bb major. Shares all seven notes. Most common minor chord in Bb major progressions.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All Bb major notes work perfectly. Natural minor sound in the key.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords in Bb major. Perfect for Bb-F-Gm-Eb progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Dorian adds E (major 6th) for brighter minor. More sophisticated than natural minor.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The E (major 6th) creates jazzy, bright minor. More modern than Aeolian.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, jazzy vi minor. Perfect for jazz and R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Bb major and G minor share all notes. G Aeolian = Bb Ionian starting on G.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize G as root. All notes work perfectly.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for understanding relative major/minor. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Natural minor sound perfect for vi minor 7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. Standard vim7 sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7 in Bb major. Perfect for Bb-F-Gm7-Eb progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple and effective. All Bb major notes work over Gm7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think Bb major, emphasize G as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic vim7 approach. Works universally.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The foundation of Western music in Bb major. Extremely common in jazz and classical.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, warm, and sophisticated. Bb is one of the most common keys for horns and woodwinds.
famousSongs: Many jazz standards and classical pieces in Bb
commonProgressions.tips: Bb major is THE key for jazz. Perfect for horn arrangements (trumpet, sax, clarinet). Can enhance with Bb-Eb-F7.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop progression in Bb. The 'Axis of Awesome' progression.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally versatile and soulful. Very common in contemporary R&B.
famousSongs: Someone Like You, With or Without You (transposed)
commonProgressions.tips: Extremely common in modern music. Works for entire songs. Can be played in any order.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic progression in Bb. The doo-wop changes.
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for retro and soul vibes. Can enhance with Bbmaj7-Gm7-Ebmaj7-F7.

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: THE fundamental jazz progression. Bb is one of the most common jazz keys.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated with strong resolution. The gold standard for jazz harmony.
famousSongs: Autumn Leaves, Satin Doll, countless jazz standards in Bb
commonProgressions.tips: MASTER this for jazz. Practice with extensions: Cm9-F13-Bbmaj9. F7 can be altered. Essential for all jazz musicians.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V
commonProgressions.description: Optimistic progression with smooth voice leading.
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for horn arrangements. Very singable. Can add 7ths for sophistication.

commonProgressions.name: vi-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Emotional pop/R&B progression starting on vi minor.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Begins melancholic, builds to hopeful resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Starting on vi creates immediate emotional impact. Great for building verses.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-ii-V
commonProgressions.description: Jazz standard progression - the rhythm changes in Bb.
famousSongs: I Got Rhythm, countless bebop heads
commonProgressions.tips: Essential for jazz. Strong harmonic motion. Add tensions for sophistication.

commonProgressions.name: vi-ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: Extended jazz progression combining relative minor with ii-V-I.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Longer journey to resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Extends ii-V-I with vi start. Practice jazz voicings. Can substitute chords (Gm7b5, F7alt).

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Modal progression in Bb borrowing bVII (Ab major).
commonProgressions.tips: The Ab is borrowed from Bb Mixolydian. Creates powerful, soulful sound.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-IV-V
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Energetic and driving. Double V creates urgency.
commonProgressions.tips: Great for high-energy sections. Repeated V emphasizes tension.

commonProgressions.name: I-iii-IV-V
commonProgressions.tips: The iii chord (Dm) is underused but effective. Creates smooth voice leading.

commonProgressions.name: 12-Bar Blues in Bb
commonProgressions.description: Classic 12-bar blues. Bb is extremely common for blues in horn keys.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: The quintessential blues sound in horn key.
commonProgressions.tips: Bb is THE blues key for horns. Use Bb blues scale and Bb minor pentatonic for solos. Essential for jazz blues.
